steps.title: Test the Oscar prediction
steps.content: The student's claim says strong opening-weekend earnings should predict Oscar recognition. Weakening evidence must show a film with stronger opening earnings failing to receive recognition while a lower-earning film did receive it.

steps.title: Ignore lifetime and director data
steps.content: B and C compare lifetime earnings, not opening-weekend earnings. D discusses directors and recognition but does not test the opening-weekend prediction. The relevant contrast shows higher opening earnings without Oscar recognition against lower opening earnings with recognition. The answer is A.
